244	\begin{theorem}\leanok
245	[Representability of the Grassmannian]
246	  \label{thm:grassmannian_representable}
247	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable}
248	  \uses{def:grassmannian_scheme, thm:relative_spec_exists, thm:relative_spec_univ}
249	
250	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
251	  % affine patches" through "Projective embedding"
252	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
253	  % L798-L930).
254	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
255	  % "For any integers \(r\ge d\ge 1\), the Grassmannian scheme
256	  %  \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\), together with the tautological
257	  %  quotient \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) where \
258	  %  \(\U\) is a rank \(d\) locally free sheaf on \(\grass(r,d)\),
259	  %  can be explicitly constructed as follows. [...]
260	  %  the schemes
261	  %  \(U^I\), as \(I\) varies over all the
262	  %  \({r\choose d}\) different
263	  %  subsets of  \(\{ 1,\ldots, r\}\) of cardinality \(d\),
264	  %  can be glued together by the co-cycle \((\theta_{I,J})\) to form a
265	  %  finite-type scheme \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\). As each \(U^I\) is
266	  %  isomorphic to \(\AA^{d(r-d)}_{\Z}\), it follows that
267	  %  \(\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z\) is smooth of relative dimension \(d(r-d)\).
268	  %  [...]
269	  %  We now show that \(\pi : \grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z\) is proper. It is enough to
270	  %  verify the valuative criterion of properness for discrete valuation rings.
271	  %  [...]
272	  %  As \(\U\) is given by the transition functions \(g_{I,J}\) described above,
273	  %  the determinant line bundle
274	  %  \(\det(\U)\) is given by the transition functions
275	  %  \(\det(g_{I,J}) = 1/P^I_J \in GL_1(U^I_J)\).
276	  %  [...]
277	  %  We leave it to the reader to verify that the sections \(\sigma_I\) form
278	  %  a linear system which is base point free and separates points
279	  %  relative to \(\spec \Z\), and so gives an embedding of
280	  %  \(\grass(r,d)\) into \(\PP^m_{\Z}\) where \(m = {r\choose d}-1\).
281	  %  This is a closed embedding by the properness of
282	  %  \(\pi:\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z\). In particular, \(\det(\U)\)
283	  %  is a relatively very ample line bundle on \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\)."
284	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Construction of Grassmannian" (FGA
285	  Explained Ch.~5).}
286	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(V\) a locally free \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module
287	  of rank \(r\), and \(1 \le d \le r\). The functor \(\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)\) of
288	  \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme} is representable by a smooth projective
289	  \(S\)-scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \to S\) of relative dimension \(d(r-d)\),
290	  equipped with a tautological quotient
291	  \(\pi^* V \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) of rank \(d\). The determinant
292	  line bundle \(\det(\mathcal{U})\) is relatively very ample, and the
293	  associated linear system gives a closed embedding
294	  \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \hookrightarrow
295	  \mathbb{P}_S\!\left(\bigwedge^d V\right)\) -- the \emph{Pl\"ucker embedding}.
296	\end{theorem}
297	
298	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the "Construction of Grassmannian" sub-section of
299	% [Nitsure], \S 1 (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
300	% L773-L930), which gives the construction in five named steps
301	% ("Construction by gluing together affine patches", "Separatedness",
302	% "Properness", "Universal quotient", "Projective embedding"). The total
303	% verbatim text is ~150 lines; it is reproduced piecewise in the
304	% SOURCE QUOTE comment of the theorem statement above.
305	\begin{proof}
306	  
307	  Over \(S = \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) and \(V = \mathcal{O}_S^{\oplus r}\) the
308	  Grassmannian \(\mathrm{Gr}(r, d)\) is constructed by gluing
309	  \(\binom{r}{d}\) affine charts \(U^I = \Spec\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) indexed by
310	  size-\(d\) subsets \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\). The chart \(U^I\)
311	  carries the matrix \(X^I\) of size \(d \times r\) whose \(I\)-th \(d \times d\)
312	  minor is the identity, with the remaining \(d(r-d)\) entries free
313	  variables (so \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\)). The
314	  transition maps \(\theta_{I,J} : U^I \cap U^J \to U^J \cap U^I\) defined
315	  by \(X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) on the locus where \(\det X^I_J\) is
316	  invertible satisfy the cocycle condition
317	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}\). The glued
318	  \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec\mathbb{Z}\) is smooth of relative dimension
319	  \(d(r-d)\). Separatedness is checked from the diagonal
320	  \(\Delta_{I,J} \subset U^I \times U^J\) cut out by \(X^J_I X^I - X^J = 0\).
321	  Properness follows from the valuative criterion for DVRs: given
322	  \(\varphi : \Spec K \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over a DVR \(R\) with fraction
323	  field \(K\), factoring through some chart \(U^I\) via a ring map
324	  \(f : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to K\), choose \(J\) minimising the valuation
325	  \(\nu(f(P^I_J))\) of the minor; on the chart \(U^J\) all entries have
326	  non-negative valuation, so \(f\) extends uniquely to \(\Spec R\).
327	
328	  The universal quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\oplus r}
329	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) is given on \(U^I\) by the matrix \(X^I\)
330	  and glued by \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I \cap U^J)\). The
331	  determinant line bundle \(\det\mathcal{U}\) has transition functions
332	  \(\det g_{I,J} = 1/P^I_J\); the global sections
333	  \(\sigma_I \in \Gamma(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), \det\mathcal{U})\) defined by
334	  \(\sigma_I|_{U^J} = P^J_I\) form a base-point-free linear system separating
335	  points over \(\Spec\mathbb{Z}\), giving an embedding into
336	  \(\mathbb{P}^{\binom{r}{d}-1}_{\mathbb{Z}}\), which is closed by
337	  properness. Representability of \(\mathrm{Grass}(r,d)\) is then verified
338	  on the universal pair \((\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), u)\): a quotient
339	  \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\oplus r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) of
340	  rank \(d\) on \(T\) is everywhere locally trivialised; on the locus where
341	  the columns indexed by \(I\) are a basis the matrix of \(q\) has the form
342	  \(X^I\), defining the classifying morphism \(T \to U^I \subset
343	  \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\). Base change from \(\mathbb{Z}\) to a general noetherian
344	  base \(S\) with a vector bundle \(V\) is direct: trivialise \(V\) on an open
345	  cover and glue. For non-trivial \(V\) the same construction produces
346	  \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)\) with Pl\"ucker embedding into
347	  \(\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)\).
348	\end{proof}

350	\section{Lean encoding}
351	\label{sec:quot_lean_encoding}
352	
353	The Lean target file is
354	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean}. The Quot-foundations
355	layer proceeds in two phases.
356	
357	\paragraph{Phase 1: Hilbert polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}).}
358	On a finite-type morphism \(\pi : X \to S\) with \(S\) noetherian and a
359	coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(X\) with proper support over \(S\), the per-fiber
360	Hilbert polynomial is encoded as a function \(s \mapsto
361	\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). Mathlib provides
362	Euler-characteristic infrastructure on coherent sheaves on noetherian
363	schemes; the polynomial property of
364	\(m \mapsto \chi(\mathcal{F} \otimes L^{\otimes m})\) requires
365	Snapper's Lemma, which is a project-side sub-build on top of the
366	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure.
367	
368	\paragraph{Phase 2: Quot and Grassmannian functors
369	(\cref{def:quot_functor}, \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme},
370	\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).} The Quot functor
371	\(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) is encoded as a contravariant functor on
372	\((\mathrm{Sch}/S)\) of \(T\)-flat coherent quotients; the Grassmannian
373	\(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d)\) is the special case \(X = S\), \(E = V\), constant
374	Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi = d\). The representing scheme
375	\(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)\) is the relative gluing of \(\binom{r}{d}\)
376	affine charts \(U^I\) (each isomorphic to a relative affine space
377	\(\mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_S\)) via the Pl\"ucker cocycle \(\theta_{I,J}\). The
378	gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData}. The
379	universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) is glued by the transition cocycle
380	\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the determinant line bundle
381	\(\det \mathcal{U}\) gives the Pl\"ucker closed embedding into
382	\(\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)\). The representability statement uses
383	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} (as in
384	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}) on the universal pair
385	\((\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d), \mathcal{U})\).
386	
387	\section{Out of scope}
388	\label{sec:quot_out_of_scope}
389	
390	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Quot-foundations layer: the Hilbert
391	polynomial, the Quot functor, and the Grassmannian scheme with its
392	representability. The following are explicitly out of scope here:
393	\begin{itemize}
394	  \item \textbf{Representability of the Quot scheme} -- the
395	    Grothendieck--Altman--Kleiman existence theorem and its proof skeleton
396	    (boundedness, Grassmannian embedding, flattening stratification,
397	    valuative criterion) are not constructed in this chapter.
398	  \item \textbf{Flat base change of cohomology} (Stacks 02KH) and its
399	    project-side substrate -- not constructed in this chapter.
400	  \item \textbf{Hilbert scheme as a separate chapter}. The Hilbert scheme
401	    \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) is the special case
402	    \(\Hilb_{X/S} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\) of the Quot functor and is
403	    introduced inline (\cref{def:quot_functor}); no separate chapter.
404	  \item \textbf{Snapper's Lemma} -- the polynomial-eventually property of
405	    Euler characteristics, used in \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Cited
406	    via Nitsure \S 1 and (textbook reference) Hartshorne III.5.2;
407	    project-side sub-build for the Lean encoding.
408	  \item \textbf{Identification with \(\mathbb{P}^n\) and relative
409	    Grassmannian variants} (Nitsure \S 1, Exercises (1)--(4)) --
410	    optional applications, not consumed by Route A.
411	  \item \textbf{Group-scheme structure on \(\Pic_{C/k}\)} (A.2.c) -- the
412	    abelian-group-scheme refinement is downstream; its blueprint home is
413	    \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}.
414	  \item \textbf{Identity-component \(\Pic^0\) and abelian-variety
415	    structure} (A.3) -- a separate Route A sub-row, fed by but not
416	    constructed in this chapter.
417	\end{itemize}

1085	  base-change map \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is the global-sections incarnation of

1141	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH (``Flat base change''), the
1142	  \(i = 0\) case.}
1143	  Consider the cartesian square
1144	  \[
1145	    \begin{array}{ccc}
1146	      X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
1147	      {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
1148	      S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
1149	    \end{array}
1150	  \]
1151	  with \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and
1152	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\). Assume that \(g\) is flat and that \(f\)
1153	  is quasi-compact and quasi-separated. Then the base-change map of
1154	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an isomorphism
1155	  \[
1156	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
1157	    f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr) = f'_*\mathcal{F}' .
1158	  \]
1159	  Equivalently, in the affine situation \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\),
1160	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat, the comparison map
1161	  \[
1162	    H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
1163	    H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
1164	  \]
1165	  is an isomorphism, where \(X_B = X \times_{\operatorname{Spec}(A)}
1166	  \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) and \(\mathcal{F}_B\) is the pullback of
1167	  \(\mathcal{F}\).
1168	
1169	  The hypothesis that \(\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent is essential: it is what
1170	  makes \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent on the affine base and thus the tilde of
1171	  a module, and it is what lets the affine reduction
1172	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}) write \(\mathcal{F}\) as
1173	  \(\widetilde{M}\) over each affine open. Without it the comparison map need not be
1174	  an isomorphism.
1175	\end{theorem}

1187	% Similarly, $R^if'_*\mathcal{F}'$ is the quasi-coherent
1188	% $\mathcal{O}_{S'}$-module associated to the $B$-module
1189	% $H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$. Since pullback by $g$ corresponds
1190	% to $- \otimes_A B$ on modules
1191	% (Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-widetilde-pullback})
1192	% we see that it suffices to prove (2).
1193	%
1194	% Let $A \to B$ be a flat ring homomorphism.
1195	% Let $X$ be a quasi-compact and quasi-separated scheme over $A$.
1196	% Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
1197	% Set $X_B = X \times_{\Spec(A)} \Spec(B)$ and denote
1198	% $\mathcal{F}_B$ the pullback of $\mathcal{F}$.
1199	% We are trying to show that the map
1200	% $$
1201	% H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \longrightarrow H^i(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
1202	% $$
1203	% (given by the reference in the statement of the lemma)
1204	% is an isomorphism.
1205	%
1206	% In case $X$ is separated, choose an affine open covering
1207	% $\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \ldots \cup U_t$ and recall that
1208	% $$
1209	% \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F}),
1210	% $$
1211	% see
1212	% Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}.
1213	% If $\mathcal{U}_B : X_B = (U_1)_B \cup \ldots \cup (U_t)_B$ we obtain
1214	% by base change, then it is still the case that each $(U_i)_B$ is affine
1215	% and that $X_B$ is separated. Thus we obtain
1216	% $$
1217	% \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) = H^p(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B).
1218	% $$
1219	% We have the following relation between the {\v C}ech complexes
1220	% $$
1221	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) =
1222	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B
1223	% $$
1224	% as follows from
1225	% Lemma \ref{lemma-affine-base-change}.
1226	% Since $A \to B$ is flat, the same thing remains true on taking cohomology."
1227	% NOTE: Lean proof is a documented sorry (deferred). Its affine input is the
1228	% affine lemma above, which in turn reduces to lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso (the
1229	% affine-pushforward-of-tilde brick) plus the tilde full-faithfulness reframe. On
1230	% top of that it needs Čech-cohomology / affine-cover infrastructure for
1231	% SheafOfModules, which Mathlib does not provide. This remains a multi-lane engine
1232	% build, not a single-iter target. See informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md.
1233	\begin{proof}
1234	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}
1235	  \emph{Reduction to the affine target.} The statement is local on \(S'\), so we
1236	  may assume \(S\) and \(S'\) are affine, say \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and
1237	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat. Because \(f\) is
1238	  quasi-compact and quasi-separated, \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent on
1239	  \(S\); it is therefore the sheaf associated to the \(A\)-module
1240	  \(H^0(S, f_*\mathcal{F}) = H^0(X, \mathcal{F})\). Likewise
1241	  \(f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) is the sheaf on \(S'\) associated to the \(B\)-module
1242	  \(H^0(X', \mathcal{F}')\). Since pullback along \(g\) corresponds to
1243	  \(- \otimes_A B\) on modules, the base-change map becomes the comparison map of
1244	  \(B\)-modules
1245	  \[
1246	    H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \longrightarrow H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B),
1247	    \qquad X_B = X \times_{\operatorname{Spec}(A)} \operatorname{Spec}(B),
1248	  \]
1249	  and it suffices to prove that this map is an isomorphism.
1250	
1251	  \medskip
1252	  \emph{The separated case via {\v C}ech complexes.} Suppose first that \(X\) is
1253	  separated over \(A\). Choose a finite affine open cover
1254	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\); by separatedness all finite
1255	  intersections \(U_{i_0 \dots i_p}\) are again affine, so {\v C}ech cohomology
1256	  computes sheaf cohomology:
1257	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F})\). Base changing
1258	  the cover yields an affine open cover
1259	  \(\mathcal{U}_B : X_B = (U_1)_B \cup \dots \cup (U_t)_B\) of the again-separated
1260	  \(X_B\), with the same property
1261	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) = H^p(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\). The
1262	  affine case (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}) identifies each
1263	  term of the base-changed {\v C}ech complex with the original term tensored over
1264	  \(A\) with \(B\); that is, there is an isomorphism of cochain complexes
1265	  \[
1266	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) \;\cong\;
1267	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B .
1268	  \]
1269	  Because \(A \to B\) is flat, \(- \otimes_A B\) is an exact functor and hence
1270	  commutes with the formation of cohomology of a complex; taking \(H^0\)
1271	  (degree-zero cohomology) gives the desired isomorphism
1272	  \(H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \cong H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\).
1273	
1274	  \medskip
1275	  \emph{The quasi-separated case.} In general \(X\) need only be quasi-separated.
1276	  Choose a finite affine open cover \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\).
1277	  Now the intersections \(U_{i_0 \dots i_p}\) are quasi-compact and separated
1278	  rather than affine, but they fall under the separated case already treated, so
1279	  flat base change holds for each of them. One then compares the two
1280	  {\v C}ech-to-cohomology spectral sequences with \(E_2\)-pages
1281	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F}))\) and
1282	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F}_B))\), converging to
1283	  \(H^{p+q}(X, \mathcal{F})\) and \(H^{p+q}(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\) respectively.
1284	  The separated case provides the term-by-term identification
1285	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F}_B)) =
1286	  \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F})) \otimes_A B\) on the
1287	  \(E_2\)-pages, and flatness of \(A \to B\) propagates this isomorphism through
1288	  the spectral sequences to the abutment. In particular, in degree \(0\) one
1289	  obtains \(H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \cong H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\), as
1290	  required. (For the \(i = 0\) statement at hand, one may avoid the spectral
1291	  sequence entirely and argue by Mayer--Vietoris induction on the number \(t\) of
1292	  cover members, reducing each step to the separated case.)
1293	\end{proof}
1294

1	\chapter{Relative Spec}
2	\label{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-172). This chapter is the foundational A.1.a sub-row of
6	% Route A (positive-genus arm of nonempty_jacobianWitness). It packages the
7	% relative-spectrum functor \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) : \mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)^{op}
8	% \to \mathrm{Sch}/X used by the relative Picard functor on a product C \times_k T.
9	% Mathlib has the absolute Spec functor and affine gluing (Scheme.GlueData,
10	% AffineScheme.glueOpens) but no general RelativeSpec construction; this chapter is
11	% the prover-ready specification for the new file AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean
12	% which iter-173 (file-skeleton lane) will scaffold.

14	\section{Setup and motivation}
15	\label{sec:relspec_setup}
16	
17	The Route~A construction of \(J = \Pic^0_{C/k}\) as a \(g\)-dimensional abelian variety is
18	organised around the relative Picard functor
19	\(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) = \Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)\)
20	on the category of \(k\)-schemes. Representing \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) requires the line-bundle
21	pullback construction on a relative curve \(C \times_k T \to T\), which in turn rests on a
22	\emph{relative scheme} construction on a quasi-coherent sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras
23	\(\mathcal{A}\), the \emph{relative spectrum}
24	\(\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X\). This chapter packages the construction
25	of \(\underline{\Spec}_X(-)\) and its core properties --- universal property and affine
26	base --- as the foundational A.1.a building block. The relative Picard
27	functor itself (A.1.c), the line-bundle pullback functor on a product (A.1.b), and the
28	identity-component / dimension theory of \(J\) (A.3) live in separate sibling chapters
29	(\S~``Out of scope'' below).
30	
31	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Section ``Relative spectrum via glueing'' and ``Relative
32	% spectrum as a functor'' (tags 01LL, 01LO, 01LQ, 01LR, 01LS, available locally at
33	% references/stacks-constructions.tex L309-L720). The Hartshorne reference is
34	% [Hartshorne], II Exercise 5.17 (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf).
35	
36	\section{Quasi-coherent sheaves of algebras}
37	\label{sec:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
38	
39	\begin{definition}\leanok
40	[Quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra]
41	  \label{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
42	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QcohAlgebra}
43	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Section 01LL ``Relative spectrum via glueing'',
44	  % Situation ``situation-relative-spec''
45	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L312-L318).
46	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
47	  % "Here \(S\) is a scheme, and \(\mathcal{A}\) is a quasi-coherent
48	  %  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebra. This means that \(\mathcal{A}\) is a
49	  %  sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebras which is quasi-coherent as an
50	  %  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module."
51	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LL (situation-relative-spec).}
52	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. A \emph{quasi-coherent sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras} is a
53	  sheaf \(\mathcal{A}\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras (i.e. an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
54	  equipped with \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear multiplication and unit morphisms satisfying
55	  associativity, commutativity, and the unit law as sheaf morphisms) whose underlying
56	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is quasi-coherent. We write \(\mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)\) for the
57	  category of quasi-coherent sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras and
58	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra morphisms.
59	\end{definition}
60	
61	% NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-174 carrier note. The iter-179
62	% Block A refactor (analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md) upgrades the carrier
63	% to a 3-field structure
64	%     `structure QcohAlgebra (X : Scheme.{u}) where`
65	%     `  sheaf        : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace`
66	%     `  unit         : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf`
67	%     `  coequifibered : NatTrans.Coequifibered`
68	%     `    (Functor.whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op unit.hom)`
69	% The new third field IS the iter-175+ overlay the prior note flagged: Mathlib
70	% commit `b80f227`'s Stacks-01LL `NatTrans.Coequifibered` predicate is strictly
71	% weaker than the full `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` claimed by Definition
72	% 1.1's prose but is equivalent for the geometric purpose (constructing the
73	% relative-spectrum scheme) via the dense-subsite equivalence
74	% `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. This is the predicate Mathlib's
75	% `AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData` consumes, so the iter-179
76	% `RelativeSpec`/`structureMorphism` bodies (now real Mathlib values) close
77	% against it.
78	
79	\section{The relative-spectrum scheme}
80	\label{sec:relspec_scheme}
81	
82	The construction is given affine-locally: on every affine open \(U = \Spec R \subseteq X\) one
83	sets \(\pi^{-1}(U) := \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))\) regarded as an \(R\)-algebra, and the local pieces
84	glue compatibly because \(\mathcal{A}\) is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
85	\(\mathcal{A}(U) \otimes_R S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}(V)\) for an inclusion
86	\(V = \Spec S \subseteq U\) gives an open immersion of the corresponding Specs).
87	
88	\begin{theorem}\leanok
89	[Relative spectrum exists]
90	  \label{thm:relative_spec_exists}
91	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec}
92	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
93	
94	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-glue-relative-spec)
95	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L381-L405).
96	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
97	  % "In Situation REF.
98	  %  There exists a morphism of schemes
99	  %  \(\)
100	  %  \pi : \underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A}) \longrightarrow S
101	  %  \(\)
102	  %  with the following properties:
103	  %  \begin{enumerate}
104	  %  \item for every affine open \(U \subset S\) there exists an isomorphism
105	  %  \(i_U : \pi^{-1}(U) \to \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))\) over \(U\), and
106	  %  \item for \(U \subset U' \subset S\) affine open the composition
107	  %  \(\)
108	  %  \xymatrix{
109	  %  \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \ar[r]^{i_U^{-1}} &
110	  %  \pi^{-1}(U) \ar[rr]^{inclusion} & &
111	  %  \pi^{-1}(U') \ar[r]^{i_{U'}} &
112	  %  \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))
113	  %  }
114	  %  \(\)
115	  %  is the open immersion of Lemma REF above.
116	  %  \end{enumerate}
117	  %  Moreover, \(\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})\)
118	  %  is unique up to unique isomorphism over \(S\)."
119	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-glue-relative-spec);
120	  cf.\ [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(a).}
121	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\mathcal{A}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra
122	  (\cref{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}). There exists a scheme
123	  \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\) together with an affine morphism
124	  \(\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X\) such that
125	  \begin{enumerate}
126	    \item for every affine open \(U \subseteq X\), there is an isomorphism
127	      \(i_U : \pi^{-1}(U) \xrightarrow{\sim} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))\) over \(U\);
128	    \item for \(U \subseteq U' \subseteq X\) both affine open, the composite
129	      $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \xrightarrow{i_U^{-1}} \pi^{-1}(U) \hookrightarrow
130	      \pi^{-1}(U') \xrightarrow{i_{U'}} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))$
131	      coincides with the open immersion induced by the restriction
132	      \(\mathcal{A}(U') \to \mathcal{A}(U)\).
133	  \end{enumerate}
134	  The pair \((\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}), \pi)\) is unique up to unique isomorphism
135	  over \(X\).
136	\end{theorem}
137	
138	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Follows immediately from
139	% Lemmas REF,
140	% REF, and
141	% REF.
142	% Uniqueness is stated in the last sentence of
143	% Lemma REF."
144	\begin{proof}
145	  
146	  
147	\leanok
148	  By the open-cover gluing principle for schemes (Mathlib
149	  \texttt{Scheme.GlueData} / \texttt{AffineScheme.glueOpens}) it suffices to specify
150	  affine local pieces \(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))\) for each affine open \(U \subseteq X\) and
151	  open-immersion transition data
152	  \(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \hookrightarrow \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))\) for \(U \subseteq U'\)
153	  satisfying the cocycle condition. The transition morphisms come from the ring maps
154	  \(\mathcal{A}(U') \to \mathcal{A}(U)\); quasi-coherence yields the cartesian square
155	  \(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) = U \times_{U'} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))\), so each transition is an
156	  open immersion. Transitivity (Stacks lemma-transitive-spec) gives the cocycle condition.
157	  Uniqueness of the glued scheme is the standard property of the gluing functor.
158	\end{proof}
159	
160	\begin{definition}
161	
162	\leanok
163	[Structure morphism of the relative spectrum]
164	  \label{def:relspec_structure_morphism}
165	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.structureMorphism}
166	  \uses{thm:relative_spec_exists, def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
167	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\mathcal{A}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra.
168	  The \emph{structure morphism}
169	  \(\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X\) is the canonical projection of the
170	  relative spectrum to its base, obtained as the colimit descent of the affine-local
171	  structure morphisms \(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \to U\) over the directed affine open
172	  cover of \(X\). It is the morphism whose affineness and base-change behaviour are the
173	  subject of the universal-property and base-change results below.
174	\end{definition}
175	
176	\begin{proof}
177	
178	\leanok
179	  Constructed directly in Lean as the base morphism of the relative gluing data of
180	  \(\mathcal{A}\); this is the projection underlying \cref{thm:relative_spec_exists}.
181	\end{proof}
182	
183	\begin{theorem}\leanok
184	[Universal property of the relative spectrum]
185	  \label{thm:relative_spec_univ}
186	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty}
187	  % NOTE iter-283: removed the spurious `thm:relative_spec_base_change` from this
188	  % \uses. The universal property is the PRIMITIVE statement, proved by reducing
189	  % to the affine-base case and gluing (Lean body of `UniversalProperty` uses
190	  % only `isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen`/`relativeGluingData`, never
191	  % `base_change`). Base change is a DOWNSTREAM corollary
192	  % (thm:relative_spec_base_change \uses thm:relative_spec_univ), so listing it
193	  % here created a univ <-> base_change dependency cycle that crashed
194	  % `leanblueprint web`.
195	  \uses{thm:relative_spec_exists, thm:relative_spec_affine_base,
196	    def:relspec_structure_morphism}
197	
198	  % NOTE (iter-173 review): the iter-173 file-skeleton encodes this as the
199	  % structural consequence ``IsAffineHom (structureMorphism 𝒜)'' rather than
200	  % the natural-bijection statement pinned by this prose. Iter-174+ must
201	  % upgrade the Lean type to a ``CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy''
202	  % witness (or equivalent) before any consumer file relies on this theorem.
203	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ continuation: Section ``Relative spectrum as
204	  % a functor'', functor F and Lemma lemma-spec
205	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L427-L466 + L547-L551).
206	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
207	  % "For any \(f : T \to S\) the pullback
208	  %  \(f^*\mathcal{A}\) is a quasi-coherent sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-algebras.
209	  %  We are going to consider pairs \((f : T \to S, \varphi)\) where
210	  %  \(f\) is a morphism of schemes and \(\varphi : f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T\)
211	  %  is a morphism of \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-algebras. Note that this is the
212	  %  same as giving a \(f^{-1}\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebra homomorphism
213	  %  \(\varphi : f^{-1}\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T\), see
214	  %  Sheaves, Lemma REF.
215	  %  This is also the same as giving an \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebra map
216	  %  \(\varphi : \mathcal{A} \to f_*\mathcal{O}_T\), see
217	  %  Sheaves, Lemma REF.
218	  %  We will use all three ways of thinking about \(\varphi\),
219	  %  without further mention. [...]
220	  %  In this way we have defined a functor
221	  %  \begin{eqnarray}
222	  %  \label{equation-spec}
223	  %  F : \Sch^{opp} & \longrightarrow & \textit{Sets} \\
224	  %  T & \longmapsto & F(T) = \{\text{pairs }(f, \varphi) \text{ as above}\}
225	  %  \nonumber
226	  %  \end{eqnarray}
227	  %  [...]
228	  %  The functor \(F\) is representable by a scheme."
229	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-spec + section-spec
230	  functor description); [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(b).}
231	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\mathcal{A}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra.
232	  Then \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\) represents the functor
233	  \(F : \Sch^{op} \to \mathrm{Set}\),
234	  $T \mapsto F(T) := \{(f, \varphi) : f : T \to X \text{ a morphism},\;
235	  \varphi : f^* \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T \text{ an } \mathcal{O}_T\text{-algebra map}\}.$
236	  Equivalently, for any \(X\)-scheme \(g : T \to X\),
237	  there is a natural bijection
238	  \[
239	    \Hom_X(T, \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})) \;\;\cong\;\;
240	    \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X\text{-alg}}(\mathcal{A}, g_* \mathcal{O}_T)
241	  \]
242	  (using the adjunction between \(g_*\) and \(g^*\) to convert between
243	  \(\mathcal{A} \to g_*\mathcal{O}_T\) and \(g^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T\)).
244	  The bijection is natural in \(T\).
245	\end{theorem}

247	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: TODO retrieve from references/stacks-constructions.tex
248	% (proof of lemma-spec, L553-L600, ~50 lines covering the Zariski sheaf property,
249	% the subfunctors F_i associated to an affine cover, and identifying each F_i
250	% with the affine-base case via lemma-spec-base-change and lemma-spec-affine).
251	% Verbatim quote omitted for length; the structural steps are restated below in
252	% project notation.
253	\begin{proof}
254	  
255	  \leanok
256	  % NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-176 placeholder-discharge note.
257	  % The iter-179 Block A refactor replaces the iter-176 placeholder bodies with
258	  % the Mathlib values ``(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData _).glued`` /
259	  % ``.toBase``; iter-179 Lane B closes the Lean ``UniversalProperty`` proof
260	  % via ``isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen`` +
261	  % ``relativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`` +
262	  % ``isAffineOpen_opensRange`` — a real proof against the gluing infrastructure,
263	  % NOT against a trivial identity placeholder. The Lean type remains the
264	  % strictly weaker ``IsAffineHom (structureMorphism 𝒜)'' rather than the
265	  % Yoneda-bijection form pinned above (iter-174+ commitment; still pending
266	  % iter-180+). Consumers may now rely on the affineness conclusion, but the
267	  % Yoneda-bijection witness is still deferred.
268	  Following Stacks tag 01LQ (proof of lemma-spec): the functor \(F\) is a Zariski sheaf
269	  because morphisms of schemes and \(\mathcal{O}\)-algebra maps both glue under open covers.
270	  Choose an affine open cover \(X = \bigcup U_i\). The subfunctor
271	  \(F_i \subseteq F\) of pairs \((f, \varphi)\) with \(f(T) \subseteq U_i\) is representable by
272	  \(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U_i))\) via the affine-base case
273	  (\cref{thm:relative_spec_affine_base}) together with the
274	  restriction identification \(F_i \cong F_{U_i,\,\mathcal{A}|_{U_i}}\).
275	  Each \(F_i \hookrightarrow F\) is an open
276	  subfunctor (pull back \(U_i\) along \(f\)). The \(F_i\) jointly cover \(F\) since the \(U_i\)
277	  cover \(X\). The gluing of representable open subfunctors produces a representing scheme,
278	  which is canonically isomorphic to the glued scheme \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\)
279	  of \cref{thm:relative_spec_exists}.
280	\end{proof}

282	\section{Affine base}
283	\label{sec:relspec_affine}
284	
285	\begin{theorem}\leanok
286	[Relative spectrum over an affine base]
287	  \label{thm:relative_spec_affine_base}
288	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.affine_base_iff}
289	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
290	  % NOTE (iter-173 review): the iter-173 file-skeleton encodes this as the
291	  % weaker ``IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜)'' rather than the canonical
292	  % iso ``Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))'' pinned by this prose. The Lean name
293	  % ``affine_base_iff'' is also misleading (the encoded type is not an iff).
294	  % Iter-174+ refines to the iff-with-Γ statement once Γ : QcohAlgebra → CommRingCat
295	  % is available.
296	  
297	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LO (lemma-spec-affine)
298	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L491-L545).
299	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
300	  % "In Situation REF.
301	  %  Let \(F\) be the functor associated to \((S, \mathcal{A})\) above.
302	  %  If \(S\) is affine, then \(F\) is representable by the
303	  %  affine scheme \(\Spec(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{A}))\)."
304	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LO (lemma-spec-affine).}
305	  Let \(X = \Spec R\) be an affine scheme and let \(\mathcal{A}\) be a quasi-coherent
306	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra. Set \(A := \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A})\), regarded as an
307	  \(R\)-algebra. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of \(X\)-schemes
308	  \[
309	    \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \;\;\cong\;\; \Spec(A).
310	  \]
311	  In particular, on an affine base the relative spectrum reduces to the absolute
312	  spectrum of the global sections.
313	\end{theorem}
314	
315	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Write \(S = \Spec(R)\) and \(A = \Gamma(S, \mathcal{A})\).
316	% Then \(A\) is an \(R\)-algebra and \(\mathcal{A} = \widetilde A\).
317	% The ring map \(R \to A\) gives rise to a canonical map
318	% \(\)
319	% f_{univ} : \Spec(A)
320	% \longrightarrow
321	% S = \Spec(R).
322	% \(\)
323	% We have
324	% \(f_{univ}^*\mathcal{A} =  \widetilde{A \otimes_R A}\)
325	% by Schemes, Lemma REF.
326	% Hence there is a canonical map
327	% \(\)
328	% \varphi_{univ} :
329	% f_{univ}^*\mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A \otimes_R A}
330	% \longrightarrow
331	% \widetilde A = \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(A)}
332	% \(\)
333	% coming from the \(A\)-module map \(A \otimes_R A \to A\),
334	% \(a \otimes a' \mapsto aa'\). We claim that the pair
335	% \((f_{univ}, \varphi_{univ})\) represents \(F\) in this case."
336	\begin{proof}
337	  
338	  \leanok
339	  % NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-176 placeholder-discharge note.
340	  % The iter-176 ``change IsAffine (Spec R); inferInstance`` form is now stale
341	  % — iter-179 Lane B closes via ``UniversalProperty 𝒜`` + ``isAffine_of_isAffineHom``
342	  % against the iter-179 Mathlib-backed ``RelativeSpec``/``structureMorphism``
343	  % bodies. The Lean type remains the strictly weaker ``IsAffine`` rather than
344	  % the canonical iso ``Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X,𝒜))'' named below (iter-174+
345	  % commitment; still pending iter-180+).
346	  Write \(X = \Spec R\) and \(A = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A})\). Quasi-coherence of
347	  \(\mathcal{A}\) gives \(\mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A}\) as \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
348	  (with the \(R\)-algebra structure on \(A\) inducing the algebra structure on
349	  \(\widetilde A\)). The ring map \(R \to A\) gives a morphism
350	  \(f_{\mathrm{univ}} : \Spec A \to \Spec R = X\) and a canonical
351	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A}\)-algebra map
352	  $\varphi_{\mathrm{univ}} : f_{\mathrm{univ}}^* \mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A \otimes_R A}
353	  \to \mathcal{O}_{\Spec A} = \widetilde A$ given by multiplication
354	  \(a \otimes a' \mapsto aa'\). We verify \((\Spec A, f_{\mathrm{univ}}, \varphi_{\mathrm{univ}})\)
355	  represents \(F\) on the affine base: given a test scheme \(T\) and a pair
356	  \((f, \varphi)\), the composite
357	  $A = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A}) \xrightarrow{f^\sharp}
358	  \Gamma(T, f^*\mathcal{A}) \xrightarrow{\varphi} \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)$
359	  is a ring map \(A \to \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)\) and hence corresponds to a morphism
360	  \(T \to \Spec A\) by the affine universal property
361	  (Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.toSpec\_naturality} /
362	  \texttt{Scheme.Hom.toSpec}). One checks this assignment inverts the universal map
363	  \((f, \varphi) \mapsto a\) defined by \(f = f_{\mathrm{univ}} \circ a\),
364	  \(\varphi = a^* \varphi_{\mathrm{univ}}\).
365	\end{proof}
366	
367	\section{Lean encoding}
368	\label{sec:relspec_lean_encoding}
369	
370	The Lean target file is the new \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean}.
371	The construction proceeds by gluing absolute affine pieces: for each affine open
372	\(U \subseteq X\) with \(U = \Spec R\) and \(A := \mathcal{A}(U)\) regarded as an
373	\(R\)-algebra, the local piece is \(\Spec A\) with structure morphism \(\Spec A \to \Spec R = U\)
374	induced by the algebra unit \(R \to A\). Mathlib provides \texttt{IsAffineOpen.toScheme} for
375	the affine-open inclusion and the absolute \texttt{Scheme.Spec} functor for the local
376	pieces; the transition isomorphisms come from quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{A}\) via
377	\texttt{QuasiCoherent.tensorIso} (or the explicit
378	\(\mathcal{A}(U) \otimes_R S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}(V)\) for \(V = \Spec S \subseteq U\)).
379	The gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData} (general construction) or
380	the convenience wrapper \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme.glueOpens}; the cocycle
381	condition reduces to Stacks \texttt{lemma-transitive-spec}. The universal property
382	(\cref{thm:relative_spec_univ}) is expressed as a
383	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} witness, providing the natural bijection
384	$\Hom_X(T, \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})) \cong
385	\Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X\text{-alg}}(\mathcal{A}, g_*\mathcal{O}_T)$ as a
386	\texttt{Yoneda}-style structure. No new core axiom is required; the entire chapter is a
387	definitional/structural construction on top of existing Mathlib affine-scheme
388	infrastructure.
389	
390	\section{Out of scope}
391	\label{sec:relspec_out_of_scope}
392	
393	This chapter packages \emph{only} the relative-spectrum construction and its core
394	properties. The following downstream constructions live in separate sibling chapters and
395	are explicitly out of scope here:
396	\begin{itemize}
397	  \item \textbf{Relative Picard functor \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\)} (A.1.c) --- consumed-by, not
398	    defined-here; its blueprint home is the planned chapter
399	    \texttt{Picard\_RelPicFunctor.tex}.
400	  \item \textbf{Line-bundle pullback on a product \(C \times_k T\)} (A.1.b) ---
401	    \texttt{Picard\_LineBundlePullback.tex} (planned).
402	  \item \textbf{Connectedness, dimensionality, smoothness of \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\)}
403	    --- downstream properties that depend on assumptions on \(\mathcal{A}\)
404	    (locally finite type, flat, etc.), not unconditional consequences of the construction.
405	  \item \textbf{Identity-component \(\Pic^0\) and abelian-variety structure} (A.3) --- a
406	    separate Route~A sub-row, fed by but not constructed in this chapter.
407	\end{itemize}

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf (`i = 0`)
10	
11	This file establishes the `i = 0` (direct-image) case of flat base change: the
12	formation of the pushforward `f_* F` of a quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat
13	base change on the target.
14	
15	Throughout we work with a (commutative, eventually cartesian) square of schemes
16	```
17	  X' --g'--> X
18	  |f'        |f
19	  v          v
20	  S' --g---> S
21	```
22	recorded by morphisms `f : X ⟶ S`, `g : S' ⟶ S`, `f' : X' ⟶ S'`, `g' : X' ⟶ X`
23	with `g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`, and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of modules on `X`.
24	
25	The three main declarations are:
26	
27	* `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap` — the canonical base-change map
28	  `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)`, built as the adjoint mate of the unit of the
29	  `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction.
30	* `AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` — for `f` affine and the
31	  square cartesian, the base-change map is an isomorphism (affine case: tensor
32	  associativity).
33	* `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` — for `g` flat and `f`
34	  quasi-compact quasi-separated, the base-change map is an isomorphism.
35	
36	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`.
37	
38	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
39	Tag 02KH.
40	-/
41	
42	universe u
43	
44	open CategoryTheory Limits
45	
46	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
47	
48	open Scheme.Modules
49	
50	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
51	  (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
52	
53	/-- The canonical base-change map for the pushforward of a sheaf of modules.
54	
55	Given a commutative square
56	```
57	  X' --g'--> X
58	  |f'        |f
59	  v          v
60	  S' --g---> S
61	```
62	(with `comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`) and a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`, this is the
63	canonical morphism `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)` of sheaves of modules on `S'`.
64	
65	It is the image, under the `(g^*, g_*)`-adjunction transpose, of the composite
66	```
67	  f_* F --f_*(unit)--> f_* (g')_* (g')^* F
68	        = (g' ≫ f)_* (g')^* F = (f' ≫ g)_* (g')^* F = g_* f'_* (g')^* F,
69	```
70	where `unit` is the unit of the `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction and the middle
71	equalities are the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward together with the
72	commutativity of the square.
73	
74	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
75	base-change diagram. -/
76	noncomputable def pushforwardBaseChangeMap (comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g) (F : X.Modules) :
77	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶
78	      (pushforward f').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) :=
79	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm
80	    ((pushforward f).map ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g').unit.app F) ≫
81	      (pushforwardComp g' f).hom.app _ ≫
82	      (pushforwardCongr comm).hom.app _ ≫
83	      (pushforwardComp f' g).inv.app _)
84	
85	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — locality of isomorphisms for `Scheme.Modules`
86	
87	Mathlib provides the per-open criterion `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
88	(`IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U)`) and the stalkwise criterion
89	`TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` for `TopCat.Sheaf`-valued morphisms,
90	but it does not package the stalk-local criterion at the level of `Scheme.Modules`
91	morphisms. The following lemmas bridge that gap; they are the locality tools needed for
92	the affine reduction of `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` (where one checks the
93	base-change map after restricting to / taking stalks over affine opens). -/
94	
95	/-- **Stalk-local criterion for isomorphisms of `𝒪_X`-modules.** A morphism `φ : M ⟶ N`
96	of sheaves of modules over a scheme `X` is an isomorphism iff its underlying morphism of
97	abelian presheaves is a stalkwise isomorphism. Project-local: Mathlib only ships the
98	per-open criterion and the `TopCat.Sheaf`-level stalk criterion separately. -/
99	theorem Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_stalkFunctor_map {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
100	    (φ : M ⟶ N) :
101	    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
102	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
103	  constructor
104	  · intro h x
105	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) := Functor.map_isIso _ _
106	    exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
107	  · intro h
108	    -- Package the underlying abelian presheaves as `TopCat.Sheaf`es.
109	    let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
110	    let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
111	    let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩
112	    haveI : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map fS.hom) := h
113	    haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
114	    have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) :=
115	      (TopCat.Sheaf.forget Ab.{u} X).map_isIso fS
116	    exact (CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)).mp h1
117	
118	/-- **Basis-local criterion for isomorphisms of `𝒪_X`-modules.** If `B` is a basis of
119	opens of `X` and `φ : M ⟶ N` restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every basic
120	open `B i`, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This reduces iso-checking from *all* opens (the
121	content of `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`) to a chosen basis. Project-local:
122	Mathlib provides the stalkwise pieces (`germ_exist_of_isBasis`,
123	`stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis`) but not the packaged criterion at the
124	`Scheme.Modules` level. -/
125	theorem Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
126	    {ι : Type*} {B : ι → X.Opens} (hB : TopologicalSpace.Opens.IsBasis (Set.range B))
127	    (φ : M ⟶ N) (h : ∀ i, IsIso (φ.app (B i))) : IsIso φ := by
128	  -- Reduce to a stalkwise isomorphism of the underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism `α`.
129	  rw [Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_stalkFunctor_map]
130	  intro x
131	  -- `α.app (op (B i))` is definitionally `φ.app (B i)`, hence an isomorphism on each basic open.
132	  have happ : ∀ U ∈ Set.range B,
133	      IsIso (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ).app (Opposite.op U)) := by
134	    rintro U ⟨i, rfl⟩; exact h i
135	  rw [CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
136	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
137	  · -- Injectivity of the stalk map from injectivity on a basis.
138	    refine TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis hB ?_ x
139	    intro U hU
140	    haveI := happ U hU
141	    exact (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso
142	      (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ).app (Opposite.op U))).injective
143	  · -- Surjectivity: a germ at `x` comes from a section over a basic open, where `α` is onto.
144	    intro t
145	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU, s, rfl⟩ :=
146	      TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist_of_isBasis hB N.presheaf x t
147	    haveI := happ U hU
148	    obtain ⟨s', hs'⟩ := (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso
149	      (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ).app (Opposite.op U))).surjective s
150	    refine ⟨M.presheaf.germ U x hxU s', ?_⟩
151	    erw [TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply]
152	    rw [hs']
153	    rfl
154	
155	/-- **Affine-open locality criterion for isomorphisms of `𝒪_X`-modules.** A morphism
156	`φ : M ⟶ N` of sheaves of modules is an isomorphism iff it restricts to an isomorphism on
157	the sections over every affine open of `X`. This is the precise reduction used in the
158	affine proof of `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`: the affine opens of `X` form a basis,
159	and over an affine open the base-change map becomes the pure ring-theoretic
160	`cancelBaseChange` isomorphism. -/
161	theorem Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
162	    (φ : M ⟶ N) : IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U : X.affineOpens, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
163	  refine ⟨fun _ U => inferInstance, fun h => ?_⟩
164	  exact Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis
165	    (B := (Subtype.val : X.affineOpens → X.Opens))
166	    (by simpa [Subtype.range_val] using X.isBasis_affineOpens) φ h

168	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — affine tilde dictionary (global sections)
169	
170	The affine reduction of `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` needs to translate
171	`Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)` of a `tilde`-module into restriction of
172	scalars. The first concrete piece is the *global-sections* identification: for a
173	ring map `φ : R ⟶ R'` and an `R'`-module `M`, the `R`-module of global sections of
174	the pushforward of `M^~` should be the restriction of scalars along `φ` of the

176	group (the global sections `Γ(M^~, ⊤)`, since `(Spec.map φ)⁻¹ᵁ ⊤ = ⊤`); the only
177	content is that the two `R`-actions agree, which is exactly the naturality of
178	`Scheme.ΓSpecIso` (`ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality`: the global-sections map of `Spec.map φ`

246	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — global-sections / pushforward ring map -/
247	
248	/-- The global-sections comparison ring map underlying `Spec.map φ` at the top open is
249	conjugate to `φ` via the global-sections isomorphisms `Γ(Spec -) ≅ -`. Concretely, the
250	square
251	```
252	  R  --gsR.hom-->  Γ(Spec R, ⊤)
253	  |φ                     |(Spec.map φ).appTop
254	  v                      v
255	  R' --gsR'.hom--> Γ(Spec R', ⊤)
256	```
257	commutes, where `gsR = StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso R`. This is the ring-level
258	heart of the affine pushforward-of-tilde identification (`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`):
259	it is exactly the `eqToIso` hypothesis required by the element-free
260	`ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'` route for the Γ-fragment iso (route (b) above), since
261	`moduleSpecΓFunctor` builds its `R`-action by `restrictScalars` along `gsR.hom` and the
262	pushforward builds its `Γ(Spec R, ⊤)`-action by `restrictScalars` along
263	`(Spec.map φ).appTop`. Project-local: Mathlib ships the underlying
264	`Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality` but not this `globalSectionsIso`/`appTop` form. -/
265	theorem globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R') :
266	    (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R).hom ≫ (Spec.map φ).appTop
267	      = φ ≫ (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R').hom := by
268	  have hR : (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R).hom = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv := rfl
269	  have hR' : (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R').hom = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R').inv := rfl
270	  rw [hR, hR']
271	  exact (Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality φ).symm
272	
273	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Γ of an affine pushforward -/
274	
275	/-- **Global sections of an affine pushforward = restriction of scalars.** For a ring
276	map `φ : R ⟶ R'` and *any* sheaf of modules `N` on `Spec R'`, the `R`-module of global
277	sections of the pushforward `(Spec φ)_* N` is the restriction of scalars along `φ` of the
278	`R'`-module of global sections of `N`. Built element-free (route (b)): both sides peel
279	(by `rfl`) to nested `ModuleCat.restrictScalars` towers over the common global-section
280	module `Γ(N, ⊤)`; the two towers are reconciled by collapsing each via
281	`ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App` and applying the ring equation
282	`globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop`. No element-level scalar multiplication is
283	touched, dodging the carrier-instance wall. Project-local: the affine companion of the
284	global-sections fragment of flat base change. -/
285	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardIso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R')
286	    (N : (Spec R').Modules) :
287	    (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj N) ≅
288	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj ((moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).obj N) := by
289	  set SecN : ModuleCat ↑((Spec R').ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
290	    (((SheafOfModules.forgetToSheafModuleCat (Spec R').ringCatSheaf (Opposite.op ⊤)
291	        (Limits.initialOpOfTerminal Limits.isTerminalTop)).obj N).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) with hSecN
292	  set gsRhom := (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R).hom.hom with hgsR
293	  set gsR'hom := (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R').hom.hom with hgsR'
294	  set pushTop := ((Spec.map φ).toRingCatSheafHom.hom.app (Opposite.op ⊤)).hom with hpush
295	  have hcomp : pushTop.comp gsRhom = gsR'hom.comp φ.hom := by
296	    apply RingHom.ext
297	    intro x
298	    simpa [hpush, hgsR, hgsR', RingHom.comp_apply] using
299	      congr($(globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop φ).hom x)
300	  exact (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App gsRhom pushTop (pushTop.comp gsRhom) rfl SecN).symm ≪≫
301	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr hcomp).app SecN ≪≫
302	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App φ.hom gsR'hom (gsR'hom.comp φ.hom) rfl SecN)
303	
304	/-- **Γ-fragment for a tilde-module.** Specialising `gammaPushforwardIso` to `N = M^~`
305	and using the unit iso `tilde.toTildeΓNatIso` (`Γ(M^~, ⊤) ≅ M`), the `R`-module of global
306	sections of `(Spec φ)_* (M^~)` is the restriction of scalars of `M` along `φ`. This is the
307	`Γ`-fragment comparison used to recognise the section-level base-change map in the affine
308	reduction of `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`. Project-local: corollary of
309	`gammaPushforwardIso`. -/
310	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardTildeIso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R')
311	    (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') :
312	    (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).obj
313	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M)) ≅
314	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M :=
315	  gammaPushforwardIso φ (tilde M) ≪≫
316	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).mapIso (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app M).symm
317	
318	/-- **Sections of an affine pushforward over an arbitrary open = restriction of scalars.**
319	The `D(a)`-level (indeed arbitrary-open) generalization of `gammaPushforwardIso`. For a ring map
320	`φ : R ⟶ R'`, an `Spec R'`-module `N`, and an open `U` of `Spec R` with preimage
321	`V = (Spec φ)⁻¹ U` in `Spec R'`, the `R`-module of sections of the pushforward
322	`(Spec φ)_* N` over `U` is the restriction of scalars along `φ` of the `R'`-module of sections of
323	`N` over `V`. Because `modulesSpecToSheaf` forgets to the *global* section ring uniformly (it
324	restricts scalars along the global-sections map at the top open, not at `U`), the construction is
325	*identical* to that of `gammaPushforwardIso` — the same restriction-of-scalars composition identity
326	twice plus the same `⊤`-level ring equation `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop` — with only
327	the evaluation open changed from `⊤` to `U` / `V`. Project-local. -/
328	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardIsoAt {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R')
329	    (N : (Spec R').Modules) (U : (Spec R).Opens) :
330	    (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj N)).val.obj
331	        (Opposite.op U) ≅
332	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj
333	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.obj
334	          (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (Spec.map φ).base).obj U))) := by
335	  set SecN : ModuleCat ↑((Spec R').ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
336	    (((SheafOfModules.forgetToSheafModuleCat (Spec R').ringCatSheaf (Opposite.op ⊤)
337	        (Limits.initialOpOfTerminal Limits.isTerminalTop)).obj N).val.obj
338	          (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (Spec.map φ).base).obj U))) with hSecN
339	  set gsRhom := (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R).hom.hom with hgsR
340	  set gsR'hom := (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R').hom.hom with hgsR'
341	  set pushTop := ((Spec.map φ).toRingCatSheafHom.hom.app (Opposite.op ⊤)).hom with hpush
342	  have hcomp : pushTop.comp gsRhom = gsR'hom.comp φ.hom := by
343	    apply RingHom.ext
344	    intro x
345	    simpa [hpush, hgsR, hgsR', RingHom.comp_apply] using
346	      congr($(globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop φ).hom x)
347	  exact (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App gsRhom pushTop (pushTop.comp gsRhom) rfl SecN).symm ≪≫
348	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr hcomp).app SecN ≪≫
349	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App φ.hom gsR'hom (gsR'hom.comp φ.hom) rfl SecN)
350	
351	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — restriction of scalars and localization -/
352	
353	/-- **Counit of the tilde–Γ adjunction is a basic-open isomorphism whenever the restriction
354	of the global sections is a localization.** For a sheaf of modules `N` on `Spec R` and `a : R`,
355	if the structure-sheaf restriction map `Γ(N, ⊤) → Γ(N, D(a))` (read in `ModuleCat R` via the
356	global-sections forgetful functor `modulesSpecToSheaf`) exhibits `Γ(N, D(a))` as the localization
357	of `Γ(N, ⊤)` at `Submonoid.powers a`, then the counit `fromTildeΓ N` is an isomorphism on the
358	sections over `D(a)`. This is the section-level engine of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`: it isolates
359	the *only* nontrivial input (`Γ(N, D(a)) = Γ(N, ⊤)[1/a]`) and discharges the rest — the comparison
360	`fromTildeΓ` is, on `D(a)`, the canonical map between two localizations of `Γ(N, ⊤)` (the tilde
361	localization `toOpen` and the restriction), hence an isomorphism by the uniqueness of localized
362	modules. Project-local: Mathlib has the localization-uniqueness lemmas but not this packaging at
363	the level of `fromTildeΓ`. -/
364	lemma fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (N : (Spec R).Modules)
365	    (a : R)
366	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
367	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.map
368	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom] :
369	    IsIso (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app N.fromTildeΓ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)) := by
370	  -- The underlying function of `Hom.app` agrees (by `rfl`) with that of the `modulesSpecToSheaf`
371	  -- image, where the tilde–Γ triangle identity `toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app` applies.
372	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
373	  have hfun : ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
374	        (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app N.fromTildeΓ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a))) =
375	      ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
376	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)))) :=
377	    rfl
378	  rw [hfun]
379	  -- Abbreviations: `j` is the tilde localization map, `ρ` the structure-sheaf restriction, both
380	  -- localizations of `Γ(N, ⊤)` at `powers a`; `L` is the section map of the counit.
381	  set j := (tilde.toOpen ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
382	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom with hj
383	  set ρ := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.map
384	    (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom with hρ
385	  haveI hρinst : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) ρ := by rw [hρ]; infer_instance
386	  clear_value ρ
387	  set L := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app
388	    (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a))).hom with hL
389	  -- The triangle identity gives `L ∘ₗ j = ρ`.
390	  have htri : L ∘ₗ j = ρ := by
391	    have := Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
392	    apply_fun ModuleCat.Hom.hom at this
393	    simpa [hL, hj, hρ, ModuleCat.hom_comp] using this
394	  -- `L` equals the canonical iso between the two localizations of `Γ(N, ⊤)`, hence bijective.
395	  set ej := IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers a) j with hej
396	  set eρ := IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers a) ρ with heρ
397	  let e := ej.symm.trans eρ
398	  have hcomp : e.toLinearMap ∘ₗ j = ρ := by
399	    ext x
400	    simp only [e, hej, heρ, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_coe,
401	      LinearEquiv.trans_apply, IsLocalizedModule.iso_symm_apply]
402	    exact IsLocalizedModule.iso_mk_one (Submonoid.powers a) ρ x
403	  have hLeq : L = e.toLinearMap :=
404	    IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers a) j (IsLocalizedModule.map_units ρ)
405	      (htri.trans hcomp.symm)
406	  rw [show ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app
407	    (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)))) = ⇑L from rfl, hLeq]
408	  exact e.bijective
409	
410	/-- **Affine pushforward of a tilde-module, conditional on the basic-open localization fact.**
411	This is the full assembly of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (route iii), modulo the single
412	remaining ingredient: that on each basic open `D(a)` the structure-sheaf restriction of the
413	pushforward `(Spec φ)_* M^~` exhibits its sections over `D(a)` as the localization at
414	`Submonoid.powers a` of its global sections. Given that hypothesis (`hloc`), the counit
415	`fromTildeΓ ((Spec φ)_* M^~)` is an isomorphism (basis-locality criterion
416	`Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis` over the basic opens, with each section iso supplied by
417	`fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule`), so `(Spec φ)_* M^~` lies in the essential image of
418	`tilde` and the global-sections comparison `gammaPushforwardTildeIso` upgrades to the desired
419	object isomorphism.
420	
421	The hypothesis `hloc` is the *only* outstanding obligation for the unconditional
422	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`: it is the affine "`(Spec φ)⁻¹ D(a) = D(φ a)`, and localizing
423	`restrict φ M` at `a` agrees with localizing `M` at `φ a`" fact (Stacks, widetilde-pullback),
424	whose ring-change core is `IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` below. Discharging it
425	requires identifying the `modulesSpecToSheaf` global-ring `R`-action on the sections of the
426	pushforward over `D(a)` with restriction of scalars along `φ` of the `R'`-localization
427	`M[1/φ a]` — the section-level structure-sheaf naturality at `D(a)`. -/
428	noncomputable def pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
429	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R')
430	    (hloc : ∀ a : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
431	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M))).val.map
432	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom) :
433	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
434	      tilde ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) := by
435	  have hiso : IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
436	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M))) := by
437	    apply Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis
438	      (B := fun a : R => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a) PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens
439	    intro a
440	    haveI := hloc a
441	    exact fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule _ a
442	  exact (asIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ _)).symm ≪≫
443	    (tilde.functor R).mapIso (gammaPushforwardTildeIso φ M)
444	
445	/-- **Restriction of scalars of a localized module is a localized module.** If `f : M →ₗ[A] N`
446	exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` at the image submonoid `Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S`
447	(for `S : Submonoid R` and `A` an `R`-algebra), then the `R`-linear map underlying `f`
448	exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` at `S` itself. This is the exact converse of
449	`IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars` and is the ring-change ingredient powering the affine
450	pushforward identification: localizing `restrictScalars φ M` at `a ∈ R` agrees with localizing
451	`M` at `φ a ∈ R'`. Project-local: Mathlib ships only the forward direction. -/
452	lemma IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars
453	    {R A : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A]
454	    {M N : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N]
455	    [Module R M] [Module R N] [Module A M] [Module A N]
456	    [IsScalarTower R A M] [IsScalarTower R A N]
457	    (S : Submonoid R) (f : M →ₗ[A] N)
458	    [IsLocalizedModule (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f] :
459	    IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R) where
460	  map_units x := by
461	    have h := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f
462	      (⟨algebraMap R A x, Submonoid.mem_map.mpr ⟨x, x.2, rfl⟩⟩ : Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S)
463	    simp only [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, Module.End.isUnit_iff] at h ⊢
464	    exact h
465	  surj y := by
466	    obtain ⟨⟨x, ⟨_, t, ht, rfl⟩⟩, e⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f y
467	    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using e⟩
468	  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} e := by
469	    obtain ⟨⟨_, c, hc, rfl⟩, h⟩ :=
470	      IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) (f := f) e
471	    exact ⟨⟨c, hc⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using h⟩
472	
473	/-- **The structure-sheaf restriction of a tilde-module from `⊤` to `D(b)` is a localization.**
474	Read in `ModuleCat R'` via `modulesSpecToSheaf`, the restriction map
475	`Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(b))` exhibits its target as the localization of its source at
476	`Submonoid.powers b`. This packages the Mathlib instance
477	`tilde.toOpen … (basicOpen b)` (which makes `M → Γ(M^~, D(b))` a localization) together with the
478	triangle identity `tilde.toOpen_res` and the fact that `M → Γ(M^~, ⊤)` is bijective (localization
479	at the trivial submonoid `powers 1`). Project-local. -/
480	lemma tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule {R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') (b : R') :
481	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b)
482	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
483	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom := by
484	  -- `toOpen M ⊤` is a localization at `powers (1 : R')` (since `basicOpen 1 = ⊤`), hence bijective.
485	  haveI inst1 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by
486	    have h := (inferInstance : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (1 : R'))
487	      (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (1 : R'))).hom)
488	    rw [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one] at h
489	    exact h
490	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by
491	    refine ⟨fun x y hxy => ?_, fun y => ?_⟩
492	    · obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq
493	        (S := Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (f := (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom) hxy
494	      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
495	      have hc1 : (↑c : R') = 1 := by simpa using hn.symm
496	      rw [Submonoid.smul_def, Submonoid.smul_def, hc1, one_smul, one_smul] at hc
497	      exact hc
498	    · obtain ⟨⟨x, c⟩, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj
499	        (Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom y
500	      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
501	      have hc1 : (↑c : R') = 1 := by simpa using hn.symm
502	      refine ⟨x, ?_⟩
503	      rw [Submonoid.smul_def, hc1, one_smul] at hc
504	      exact hc.symm
505	  -- Triangle identity: `toOpen ⊤ ≫ (restriction) = toOpen (D b)`.
506	  have htri := tilde.toOpen_res M (⊤ : (Spec R').Opens) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)
507	    (homOfLE (le_top))
508	  -- The inverse equivalence `Γ(M^~, ⊤) ≃ M`.
509	  set le : M ≃ₗ[R'] ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
510	    LinearEquiv.ofBijective (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom hbij with hle
511	  -- The triangle at the level of linear maps: `restriction ∘ₗ le = toOpen (D b)`.
512	  have htri2 : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
513	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom ∘ₗ le.toLinearMap
514	      = (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom := by
515	    have h := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom htri
516	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp] at h
517	    exact h
518	  -- Hence `restriction = toOpen (D b) ∘ₗ le⁻¹`, a localization at `powers b` precomposed with
519	  -- a linear equivalence.
520	  have key : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
521	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom
522	      = (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom ∘ₗ le.symm.toLinearMap :=
523	    (LinearEquiv.eq_comp_toLinearMap_symm _ _).mpr htri2
524	  rw [key]
525	  exact IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (Submonoid.powers b)
526	    (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom le.symm
527	
528	/-- **Affine pushforward of a tilde-module (unconditional).** For a ring map `φ : R ⟶ R'`
529	and an `R'`-module `M`, pushing the quasi-coherent sheaf `M^~` forward along `Spec φ` is,
530	up to canonical isomorphism, the tilde of the restriction of scalars of `M` along `φ`. This
531	is the unconditional form of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule`: the latter's
532	hypothesis `hloc` (each structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(N,⊤) → Γ(N,D(a))` is a localization at
533	`powers a`) is discharged here by the `D(a)`-level transport. See blueprint
534	`lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`. -/
535	noncomputable def pushforward_spec_tilde_iso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
536	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') :
537	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
538	      tilde ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) := by
539	  apply pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule φ M
540	  intro a
541	  -- STRATEGY (`of_linearEquiv`): the restriction map `ρ : Γ(N,⊤) → Γ(N,D(a))` of
542	  -- `N := (Spec φ)_* (M^~)` is, under the isomorphisms
543	  -- `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) ⊤` and `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) (D a)`,
544	  -- identified with the restriction of scalars along `φ` of the `R'`-side tilde restriction
545	  -- `σ : Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(φ a))`. That `σ` is a localization at `powers (φ a)`
546	  -- (`tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule`), so its restriction of scalars along `φ` is a
547	  -- localization at `powers a` (`IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars`); transporting along
548	  -- the two isomorphisms then yields `hloc(a)`.
549	  -- The two bricks for this discharge are now in place and axiom-clean:
550	  --   • `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) U : Γ(N, U) ≅ restrictScalars φ (Γ(M^~, (Spec φ)⁻¹ U))`
551	  --     — the open-indexed `e_{D(a)}` isomorphism (blueprint movement (1)); and
552	  --   • `tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule M (φ a)` — the `R'`-side restriction
553	  --     `Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(φ a))` is a localization at `powers (φ a)`.

654	/-- **Naturality of the Γ-fragment comparison.** The per-object isomorphisms
655	`gammaPushforwardIso φ N : Γ_R((Spec φ)_* N) ≅ restrictScalars φ (Γ_{R'} N)` assemble into a
656	natural isomorphism of functors `(Spec R').Modules ⥤ ModuleCat R`
657	\[ (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \;\circ\; \Gamma_R
658	   \;\cong\; \Gamma_{R'} \;\circ\; \operatorname{restr}_\varphi . \]
659	Every constituent of `gammaPushforwardIso` is the identity on underlying elements (the
660	`restrictScalarsComp'App` isos and `restrictScalarsCongr` merely repackage the module structure
661	on the unchanged carrier), so naturality is a pointwise `rfl`. This is the right-adjoint natural
662	isomorphism that drives the affine pullback dictionary `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` via
663	`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso`. Project-local. -/
664	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardNatIso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R') :
665	    Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R) ≅
666	      moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R') ⋙ ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom :=
667	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun N => gammaPushforwardIso φ N) (by
668	    intro N N' g
669	    ext x
670	    rfl)
671	
672	/-- **Affine pullback of a tilde-module.** For a ring map `φ : R ⟶ R'` and an `R`-module `M`,
673	pulling the quasi-coherent sheaf `M^~` back along `Spec φ` is, up to canonical isomorphism, the
674	tilde of the extension of scalars `R' ⊗_R M`. This is the pullback companion of
675	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` and is part (1) of Stacks Tag 01I9 (`lemma-widetilde-pullback`).
676	
677	The construction is the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints route. Pullback along `Spec φ` is left adjoint
678	to pushforward, and `tilde` is left adjoint to global sections; their composite
679	`tilde_R ⋙ pullback` is left adjoint to `pushforward ⋙ Γ_R`. Symmetrically `extendScalars φ` is
680	left adjoint to `restrictScalars φ`, so `extendScalars ⋙ tilde_{R'}` is left adjoint to
681	`Γ_{R'} ⋙ restrictScalars φ`. The two right adjoints are identified by the natural isomorphism
682	`gammaPushforwardNatIso` (which is the global-sections comparison `gammaPushforwardIso`), so
683	`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso` yields an isomorphism of the two left adjoints; evaluating
684	at `M` gives the claim. Project-local: the affine companion of the pushforward dictionary. See
685	blueprint `lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso`. -/
686	noncomputable def pullback_spec_tilde_iso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
687	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) :
688	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
689	      tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars φ.hom).obj M) :=
690	  let adjL := (tilde.adjunction (R := R)).comp
691	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map φ))
692	  let adjR := (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj φ.hom).comp (tilde.adjunction (R := R'))
693	  (((conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR).symm (gammaPushforwardNatIso φ)).symm).app M
694	
695	/-- **Affine base change.** If `f` is an affine morphism and the square is
696	cartesian, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an isomorphism. In the
697	affine-local picture this is the associativity isomorphism
698	`(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M ≅ R' ⊗_R M`, which needs no flatness.
699	
700	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma "Affine base change". -/
701	theorem affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
702	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
703	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by
704	  -- A morphism of sheaves of modules is an isomorphism iff it is an isomorphism
705	  -- on sections over every *affine* open of the base `S'` (locality criterion
706	  -- `Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens`; the affine opens form a basis).

710	  intro U
711	  -- Remaining goal: `IsIso (Hom.app (pushforwardBaseChangeMap …) U)` for `U` affine.

714	  --   • pushforward — `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso φ M : (Spec φ)_* (M̃) ≅ (restrictScalars φ M)~`

719	  --   • the pure ring-theoretic target `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange`
720	  --     `(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M ≃ R' ⊗_R M` (no flatness) is in Mathlib.

8	/-!
9	# Generic flatness of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)
10	
11	This file packages the classical Grothendieck/Nitsure~\S4 **generic flatness**
12	theorem: over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent `𝓞_X`-module on a
13	finite-type morphism `X ⟶ S` becomes flat after restricting to a non-empty open
14	subscheme of `S`. This is the inductive engine underlying the
15	flattening-stratification construction of Grothendieck's existence theorem for
16	the Quot scheme.
17	
18	Each blueprint-pinned declaration carries the *intended* substantive type
19	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
20	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`) with a `sorry`
21	body where the proof is not yet supplied. The substantive generic-flatness
22	proof is deep (Nitsure~\S4: prime-filtration dévissage + Noether normalisation +
23	clearing denominators).
24	
25	The blueprint-pinned declaration is:
26	
27	- `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (theorem) — **generic flatness**
28	  [Nitsure §4 Theorem]: over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf
29	  on a finite-type `X ⟶ S` is flat over a non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. Its
30	  algebraic form (`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin) is the
31	  statement that a finite module over a finite-type algebra over a noetherian
32	  domain becomes free after inverting one non-zero element of the base.
33	
34	The file-internal `GenericFreeness` namespace lands the **finite-module /
35	finite-morphism special case** of the algebraic form axiom-clean, as a reusable
36	building block for the dévissage's leaves.
37	
38	## Note on type expressivity
39	
40	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
41	`genericFlatness` requires the existence of a *non-empty* open `V` and
42	substantive flatness on every affine `U ⊆ V`; both quantifiers are necessary
43	(without them the statement collapses to `V = ∅`).
44	
45	## Mathlib status
46	
47	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides `Module.Flat`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat`
48	(morphism-level), `IsImmersion`, `IsLocallyNoetherian`, `IsIntegral`,
49	`LocallyOfFiniteType`, and the dévissage / Noether-normalisation infrastructure
50	the algebraic generic-flatness argument consumes. It does NOT yet provide the
51	polynomial-ring core of generic freeness, nor the geometric `genericFlatness`
52	statement packaging it over a noetherian integral base.
53	
54	## References
55	
56	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`.
57	Source: Nitsure, "Construction of Hilbert and Quot schemes", §4 (FGA Explained
58	Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–18); Stacks Project tag 00HB (module flat).
59	-/
